heading: Reporting, payment, and allocation of premium taxes
label: 8201 Reporting, payment, and allocation of premium taxes

children.heading: Home State’s exclusive authority
children.label: a Home State’s exclusive authority
children.text: No State other than the home State of an insured may require any premium tax payment for nonadmitted insurance.

children.heading: Allocation of nonadmitted premium taxes
children.label: b Allocation of nonadmitted premium taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The States may enter into a compact or otherwise establish procedures to allocate among the States the premium taxes paid to an insured’s home State described in subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: if adopted on or before the expiration of the 330-day period that begins on , shall apply to any premium taxes that, on or after , are required to be paid to any State that is subject to such compact or procedures; andJuly 21, 2010July 21, 2010

children.label: B
children.text: if adopted after the expiration of such 330-day period, shall apply to any premium taxes that, on or after January 1 of the first calendar year that begins after the expiration of such 330-day period, are required to be paid to any State that is subject to such compact or procedures.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Upon the expiration of the 330-day period referred to in paragraph (2), the NAIC may submit a report to the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate identifying and describing any compact or other procedures for allocation among the States of premium taxes that have been adopted during such period by any States.

children.heading: Nationwide system
children.label: 4 Nationwide system
children.text: The Congress intends that each State adopt nationwide uniform requirements, forms, and procedures, such as an interstate compact, that provide for the reporting, payment, collection, and allocation of premium taxes for nonadmitted insurance consistent with this section.

children.heading: Allocation based on tax allocation report
children.label: c Allocation based on tax allocation report
children.text: To facilitate the payment of premium taxes among the States, an insured’s home State may require surplus lines brokers and insureds who have independently procured insurance to annually file tax allocation reports with the insured’s home State detailing the portion of the nonadmitted insurance policy premium or premiums attributable to properties, risks, or exposures located in each State. The filing of a nonadmitted insurance tax allocation report and the payment of tax may be made by a person authorized by the insured to act as its agent.
